prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: Fukui reactivity indices for aniline – largest electrophilic Fukui function value f⁺ (units: electrons, dimensionless).
• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis (tier `standard`), density-fitted, PySCF 2.x backend.
• Geometry provenance: fixed geometry taken from user-supplied file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz”; no re-optimization at DFT (single-point N, N+1, and N–1 charge states). Thus the calculation is B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input-XYZ.
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N; cation (+1, 2) and anion (–1, 2) states also computed internally for the finite-difference Fukui scheme.
• Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations reported convergence; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true. Density fitting (RIJK) employed by PySCF.
• Engine & version: chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill (PySCF driver).

Live log & files
• The run wrote its live log to a file announced at launch (path preserved in session history); final JSON result: “/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpod10ns5f/live.json”.
• Generated plot of atom-mapped Fukui values: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_o3/20260703-190629_aniline_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png

Headline result
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2 in zero-based counting)
  ‑ f⁺ = 2.2975867 e (dimensionless electrons) ← reported above as headline numeric value
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (N, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.20452359 e
